title: persistence contract + resume-after-crash
manager: Machines survive process death — tapes and snapshots live in durable storage, and restart means rehydrate the snapshot, replay the tape tail, and continue exactly where you were. This is a durable-execution engine (the thing Temporal sells) for the price of a contract, because FSL's replay already makes a run reproducible; persistence just gives that run somewhere to survive a crash and pick back up.
cs: pluggable store API (append tape segment, write snapshot, read-latest); resume = deserialize snapshot (version-gated) -> replay tape suffix per C2 -> continue; crash-mid-macrostep re-executes from last commit (exactly-once state, at-least-once effects); snapshot cadence configurable; fsl-twin of jssm#813.
